user_query: Analyze potentiostatic electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (PEIS) data to calculate the ionic conductivity of a material. Load the experimental PEIS data from the path specified by os.getenv('PEIS_DATA_PATH'). This file also provides the sample height and diameter. Use the impedance package with the CPE1-p(CPE2, R1) equivalent circuit model. You need to use a global optimization approach to find good initial parameter guesses before final circuit fitting. Output the final ionic conductivity value in S/cm units.
solution_code_or_process: ionic_conductivity.py, Remember to set the PEIS_DATA_PATH environment variable to yourabsolutepath/deep_solver_benchmark/data_for_demonstration/Li2Fe0p8Ni0p2Cl4_PEIS.json and source ~/.bashrc, so the agents can access the experimental data.
notes: All the contents in the agent_answer_o3 folder's files are automatically generated by the agents of OpenAI o3 model, except for the Nyquist plot visualization and performance metrics code at the end which we added to evaluate the quality of the fit. From the results, the agent's algorithm runs faster and fits better than our solution code, producing more accurate results.
